btts_and_over2.5_119428074.maps_to: Q4 - direct match (BTTS=Yes AND total goals >2.5 == BTTS AND 3+ goals)

qatar_over_under_0.5_goals_119428092.maps_to: Q7 - direct match (Qatar scores >=1 goal == Over 0.5)

player_sot_over0.5_144466056.maps_to: Q9 (Afif), Q10 (Xhaka) - direct match, full-match player 1+ SOT

switzerland_over_under_0.5_cards_1194282.note: FULL-MATCH Switzerland 1+ card. Q3 asks specifically about 2ND HALF - no such market exists. Used as an anchor for the Poisson half-split model below.

both_teams_to_score_119428046.note: context/cross-check for Q4, consistent with BTTS&O2.5 joint price (positive correlation confirmed)

structural_gaps_confirmed: No team-level 'shots on target over/under' market for Qatar (full match or 2nd half) anywhere in the 173-market enumeration - Q5 and Q8 are structural gaps, same pattern as CAN-BIH Q2.
No 'offside' market of any kind - Q2 is a structural gap, same pattern as USA-PAR Q1.
No team-fouls-comparison market - Q6 is a structural gap, same CONFIRMED PERMANENT pattern as CAN-BIH Q1.
No '2nd half team cards' market for either team - Q3 has no direct market, only the full-match Switzerland cards market as an indirect anchor.
No 'halftime shots on target' market for either team - Q1 (compound) is a structural gap.

polymarket_gamma_api.note: First search used the wrong slug pattern ('fifwc-qat-sui...') - 'sui' should have been 'che' (Switzerland=CHE, ISO code). Real event found, $4.49M volume. Only winner/draw + halftime-result + exact-score sub-markets exist - no BTTS/goals-total/SOT/fouls/cards/offsides markets. KEY NEW SIGNAL: Switzerland is a HEAVY favorite (81.5%) - more lopsided than we'd assumed when building the tier2 models. This reinforces the qualitative 'Qatar pinned back, late/2H-only chances' adjustment used for Q1/Q5/Q8, and supports treating Qatar as a stronger underdog than in their El Salvador/Ireland reference matches.

kalshi.method: api.elections.kalshi.com, series KXWCGAME-26JUN13QATSUI / KXWCBTTS / KXWC1HTOTAL / KXWCTEAMTOTAL
kalshi.note: INDEPENDENT 2nd source for Q7: Kalshi 'Qatar Over 0.5 goals' = 39.5%, essentially identical to Smarkets' 39.86% - cross-verified, upgrades Q7 confidence. Kalshi plain BTTS (39.5%) > our Q4 BTTS-AND-Over2.5 (32%), directionally consistent (the AND condition is a subset). No SOG/fouls/cards/offsides series found for this event - structural gaps confirmed by a 3rd exchange.

betfair_exchange.note: 403 geo/IP block ('country Betfair does not accept bets from'), also failed via r.jina.ai proxy (429). Could not check for Betfair's typically-richer prop market set (team SOT, offside specials, half-by-half cards).

oddschecker_via_jina.note: Total goals Over 2.5 at -145 (~59% implied pre-vig) - close cross-check vs Smarkets full-match Over2.5 mid (58.66%). A Granit Xhaka 'Over 0.5 assists' market exists (+330, ~23%) but no SOT market for Xhaka was found here. No BTTS-combo, Qatar-goals, team-SOT, offsides, fouls, or 2H-cards markets found.

summary_stats.note_2h_sot: Both of Qatar's only SOT events (1 per match) occurred in the 2nd half (71', 90+4') - n=2, but consistent with a team that sits deep and creates only late chances.

caveats: n=2 only - the 4 older 2025 AFC-region friendlies (vs Zimbabwe, Russia, Bahrain, Lebanon) had no boxscore stats via this endpoint, only key events.
footystats.org Qatar team page did not load season aggregates in the time available - no independent cross-check.
Both reference matches (El Salvador, Rep. of Ireland) are reasonable underdog-role comparables for facing Switzerland.

footystats_qatar_n10.note: Larger (n=10) season-aggregate sample for Qatar fouls, notably LOWER than the n=2 ESPN estimate (13.0/match). Does not break out SOT or half-splits (premium-gated). This materially narrows the Qatar-vs-Switzerland foul-rate gap used in Q6 (9.3 vs Switzerland's 8.875, nearly even) vs the original n=2-driven 13.0 vs 8.875.

espn_qatar_wc2022_realdata.note: Qatar as host playing 3 group matches vs decent-to-strong opposition (lost all 3, first host ever to do so). Combined with the existing n=2 (El Salvador, Ireland), gives an n=5 'vs strong/European-or-South-American opposition' sample: fouls=[10,16,15,7,9] avg=11.4, full SOT=[1,1,0,3,3] avg=1.6, 2H SOT=[1,1,0,3,1] avg=1.2. This is now the primary sample for Q1/Q5/Q8/Q6.

espn_opponent_vs_qatar_realdata.method: site.api.espn.com boxscores include BOTH teams' statistics - extracted the OPPONENT's offsides/fouls from the same 10 matches used for espn_qatar_wc2022_realdata and espn_qatar_afc2024_realdata, i.e. 'what stats does a team rack up when playing AGAINST Qatar's specific defense'
espn_opponent_vs_qatar_realdata.note: KEY FINDING for Q2: teams playing AGAINST Qatar's deep/disorganized low block average 2.5 offsides/match (n=10), and in the 3 WC2022 matches where Qatar sat deepest as a massive underdog (most analogous to today), opponents got 3, 4, 5 offsides - ALL >=2. This REVERSES the prior qualitative assumption that a deep block reduces opponent offsides. Secondary finding for Q6: opponents commit fouls at 15.2/match vs Qatar (consistent across both WC2022 and AFC2024) - much higher than Switzerland's own 8.875 friendly/qualifier baseline, suggesting a competition-context effect (see espn_switzerland_tournament_realdata).

espn_switzerland_tournament_realdata.method: site.api.espn.com - Switzerland's 9 major-tournament matches (WC2022 group+R16, Euro2024 group+R16+QF), to test whether Switzerland's 8.875 fouls/8 friendly-and-qualifier-match baseline holds in tournament context
espn_switzerland_tournament_realdata.note: KEY FINDING for Q6: Switzerland's tournament-context foul rate (12.56/match, n=9) is 41% HIGHER than their friendly/qualifier rate (8.875, n=8) - same competition-context-inflation pattern already flagged for Switzerland's card rate (0.625 friendly vs ~1.3 implied by Smarkets' WC market). Switzerland's tournament-context OFFSIDES (1.67) barely moves from their friendly rate (1.5), confirming the Q2 'opponent vs Qatar' offside finding is a genuine Qatar-specific effect, not just tournament inflation. For Q6: in the apples-to-apples WC2022-only comparison, Switzerland (13.25, n=4) actually fouls MORE than Qatar (10.33, n=3) - the opposite direction of the original session-1/2/3 estimates, which were built on a competition-context mismatch (Qatar's tournament-heavy sample vs Switzerland's friendly-heavy sample).

espn_qatar_afc2024_realdata.method: site.api.espn.com AFC Asian Cup 2024 (Qatar won as host)
espn_qatar_afc2024_realdata.note: n=7 vs weaker AFC opposition: avg fouls=12.7, avg SOT=4.7 (~3x the strong-opposition rate). KEY FINDING: fouls rate (12.7) is similar to or HIGHER than the strong-opposition rate (11.4) - Qatar's foul rate appears roughly opponent-independent (~11-13/match), unlike SOT which is heavily opponent-dependent. This argues the footystats n=10 figure (9.3) may be a stale/different-window outlier vs 3 independent real-match samples (ESPN n=2=13.0, WC2022 n=3=10.33, AsianCup n=7=12.7) all clustering 10-13.

current_2026_findings.lineups: Both Afif (Qatar) and Xhaka (Switzerland, captain, double-pivot w/ Freuler) expected to start, per pre-match previews dated ~2026-06-10. No injury/availability concerns reported for either - 'fully fit squad' per Lopetegui.
current_2026_findings.qatar_form: Qatar 'scoreless for 373 minutes across competitions, 1 goal in last 5 games' (last goal vs Syria, Arab Cup Dec 2025). Under new coach Lopetegui, Qatar has won only 2 of 11 games. Opta gives Switzerland ~76% win prob (vs Polymarket's 81.5%), median predicted score 2-0 Switzerland.
current_2026_findings.tactical_narrative: Consensus: Qatar plays a deep low block reliant on Afif counters; Switzerland dominates possession via Xhaka/Freuler pivot and probes via wide play/crosses. Supports the 'Qatar pinned back, late/sparse chances' framing already used for Q1/Q5/Q8.
current_2026_findings.referee: One source named Said Martinez (Honduras, career ~4.3 YC/game - moderately card-heavy) but with a conflicting kickoff date (listed as June 12) - FLAGGED UNCONFIRMED, not used to adjust Q3.

historical_2022_context.squad_continuity: Core players (Afif, Khoukhi, Pedro Miguel, Al Haydos) return from the 2022 WC squad, but coach changed (Felix Sanchez -> Lopetegui in 2026). 2022-era data treated as relevant-but-slightly-stale comparable.
historical_2022_context.implication: Current 2026 attacking form ('373 min scoreless') appears WORSE than the 2022 WC sample average (which included a 3-SOT match vs Senegal and a 3-SOT match vs Netherlands) - argues for weighting the n=2 2026 matches (avg SOT=1.0) somewhat more than a flat n=5 average (avg SOT=1.6) when estimating Q5/Q8.

matches.note: both Qatar goals (49', 74') came in 2nd half

alt_qatar_matches_search_summary.note: 2 additional Qatar matches with higher SOT (3, 4) than the ESPN n=2 (1, 1) - but BOTH are vs weaker AFC/Arab opposition (Tunisia, UAE), unlike today's opponent Switzerland (a strong European side, 81.5% win prob per Polymarket). Opponent-quality mismatch means these are lower-relevance comparables, but they do show Qatar's SOT ceiling is higher than n=2-vs-Europe suggested, and that even vs weaker opposition, goals/chances cluster in 2H (consistent pattern, now n=4/4 'late chances').

conflicts: vs Sweden (2025-11-15): UEFA official report says Switzerland fouls=18 total (9 in 1H) vs ESPN-derived figure of 13 - genuine source conflict, UEFA likely more authoritative but full report (PDF) was 403/503-blocked so 1H/2H split unconfirmed.
vs Sweden (2025-11-15): UEFA report says Switzerland offsides=0 - matches our existing ESPN figure (0), no conflict here.
vs Australia (2026-06-06): one search summary says offsides '3 each' (both teams) vs our ESPN figure of Switzerland=4 - minor discrepancy.
vs Norway (2026-03-31): one source says offsides=2 vs our ESPN figure of 1 - minor discrepancy.
switzerland_data_quality_conflicts.note: Sofascore, WhoScored, FBref, Transfermarkt, and UEFA PDF reports were all blocked (403/503) even via r.jina.ai proxy - could not get independent per-half splits. Net effect: Switzerland's offside/foul numbers have +/-1-5 noise across sources but no large systematic shift; ESPN figures retained as primary, ranges noted.

caveats: Offsides highly variable (range 0-4); last 2 matches (4,3) notably higher than the earlier run (avg 0.75) - recency vs full-sample tension.

Card data shows much LOWER full-match card rate (0.625/match, implies ~46.5% P(>=1 card)) than the live Smarkets 'Switzerland over 0.5 cards' market (72.7% mid) - large divergence between friendly/qualifier sample and World Cup-match pricing, likely reflects higher-stakes/host-crowd intensity not captured in the sample.

157a5c1f-b769-49c6-aec9-c305df9d55de.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?

6bf2d51d-eda0-48eb-a902-e3a1fb5b7ca6.question: Will Qatar score at least 1 goal?
6bf2d51d-eda0-48eb-a902-e3a1fb5b7ca6.source: Smarkets Qatar Over 0.5 goals (0.3986, VERIFIED_API) + Kalshi Qatar Over 0.5 goals (0.395, VERIFIED_API) - two independent exchanges agree almost exactly + social-media current-form context (UNVERIFIED_SEARCH_SUMMARY)
6bf2d51d-eda0-48eb-a902-e3a1fb5b7ca6.note: Cross-verified across 2 independent exchanges at ~0.40 (session 2). SMALL DOWNWARD ADJUSTMENT (session 3, 0.40->0.38) given fresh qualitative signal: Qatar 'scoreless for 373 minutes across competitions, 1 goal in last 5 games' under a new manager (2/11 record). Two sharp markets likely already price most of this in, so this is a small nudge rather than a full override - per the crowd-bias memo, deviations from a cross-verified market need real justification, and this is a downward deviation (away from crowd inflation, not toward it), unlike the KOR-CZE penalty case that lost from an unjustified upward deviation.

5214295f-dbe0-4d7a-839d-434b82b73753.question: Will Akram Afif have at least 1 shot on target?
5214295f-dbe0-4d7a-839d-434b82b73753.source: Smarkets player SOT>0.5 (VERIFIED_API, offer-only/illiquid - same pattern as Dzeko/Balogun)
5214295f-dbe0-4d7a-839d-434b82b73753.note: Per RULE 2, stick close to the market-implied number undeviated; shaded 0.7pp below the offer-only price as a slight conservatism adjustment (precedent: Dzeko/Balogun offer-only prices used near-verbatim worked fine, +2.89/+2.38 RBP). Reliability medium (not high) because it's offer-only/illiquid - no real bid to confirm. Session 3: social-media research confirms Afif expected to start (Qatar's primary attacking outlet/counter threat) - removes 'will he even play' uncertainty but doesn't change the number, since Qatar's overall attacking output (Q8 lambda~1.35 team SOT) is consistent with an individual lambda~0.5-0.7 for Afif specifically.

96096e67-2fae-46e0-ae55-dc21beffb541.question: Will Granit Xhaka have at least 1 shot on target?
96096e67-2fae-46e0-ae55-dc21beffb541.note_reliability: Direct market match, but two-sided spread is wide (37.0% bid to 45.5% offer, 8.4pp) - less liquid than Q4/Q7. Session 3: social-media research confirms Xhaka starting as captain in the Xhaka-Freuler double pivot - removes lineup risk but doesn't change the number (a deep-lying midfielder's SOT rate is naturally lower-variance than a forward's, consistent with the 0.41 market price).

b833736f-2ceb-43d8-9d34-f5889d1d3179.question: Will Qatar commit more fouls than Switzerland?
b833736f-2ceb-43d8-9d34-f5889d1d3179.estimate: MAJOR REVERSAL (session 3, part 2). The 0.58-0.72 range across sessions 1-3 all compared Qatar's TOURNAMENT-HEAVY foul sample (n=12, avg~11.5-12.16) against Switzerland's FRIENDLY/QUALIFIER sample (n=8, avg=8.875) - a competition-context mismatch. New data: pulled Switzerland's own 9 major-tournament matches (WC2022 n=4 + Euro2024 n=5) - Switzerland's TOURNAMENT foul rate is 12.56/match (n=9), 41% higher than their friendly rate, mirroring the same context-inflation already seen in Switzerland's card rate. Recomputing Skellam under different framings: (a) WC2022-ONLY same-tournament (Qatar n=3=10.33 vs Swiss n=4=13.25): P(diff>0)=0.274 [Swiss foul MORE]. (b) All-tournament (Qatar n=10=12.0 vs Swiss n=9=12.56): P=0.455 [near coin-flip, Swiss slightly more]. (c) All-data blend (Qatar n=12=11.5 vs Swiss n=17=10.82): P=0.557 [Qatar slightly more]. (d) Original session-3 mismatched framing: P=0.719. Weighted toward the apples-to-apples comparisons (0.2*0.274 + 0.4*0.455 + 0.4*0.557 = 0.46).
b833736f-2ceb-43d8-9d34-f5889d1d3179.source: ESPN site API (VERIFIED_API, Qatar n=2+3+7=12 / Switzerland friendly n=8 + Switzerland tournament n=9=WC2022+Euro2024) + Skellam model across multiple context-framings
b833736f-2ceb-43d8-9d34-f5889d1d3179.note: Structural gap confirmed across Smarkets, Kalshi, and Polymarket - no team-fouls-comparison market exists anywhere (consistent CONFIRMED PERMANENT pattern from CAN-BIH). MAJOR REVERSAL from session-3-part-1's 0.66 (and sessions 1/2's 0.58-0.65) - those were all built on a Qatar-tournament-vs-Switzerland-friendly mismatch. Once corrected to tournament-vs-tournament, Switzerland's WC2022 foul rate (13.25) actually EXCEEDS Qatar's (10.33). Landed near coin-flip (0.46), reliability downgraded medium->low given how sensitive this estimate is to the context-framing choice (range 0.27-0.72 across 4 reasonable framings) - this is the least-certain estimate in the match.

1b3d100e-f943-432c-93d4-9e900d641d97.question: Will Switzerland be caught offside 2 or more times?
1b3d100e-f943-432c-93d4-9e900d641d97.estimate: MAJOR REVISION (session 3). Prior estimate (0.42) assumed a deep Qatar low block REDUCES opponent offsides. New data DIRECTLY TESTS this: re-extracted the OPPONENT's offside counts from the same 10 ESPN boxscores already pulled for Qatar's own stats (espn_opponent_vs_qatar_realdata). Result: teams playing AGAINST Qatar average 2.5 offsides/match (n=10), and in the 3 WC2022 matches (Qatar deepest/biggest underdog, most analogous to today) opponents got 3, 4, 5 - ALL >=2. The deep-block assumption was BACKWARDS - a disorganized low block produces MORE offside situations (late/mistimed attacking runs against a back line that intermittently steps up), not fewer. Blending Switzerland's own recency-weighted rate (lambda=2.0, n=4, last 4 matches) with the Qatar-specific opponent effect (lambda=2.5, n=10): lambda_blend=(2.0+2.5)/2=2.25 -> Poisson P(X>=2)=1-e^-2.25*(1+2.25)=0.6575. Shaded slightly down for n=3/n=10 sample noise and AFC2024 opponents not being stylistically like Switzerland.
1b3d100e-f943-432c-93d4-9e900d641d97.source: ESPN site API real per-match data for Switzerland (VERIFIED_API, n=8) + opponent-vs-Qatar boxscore re-extraction (VERIFIED_API, n=10) + Poisson model
1b3d100e-f943-432c-93d4-9e900d641d97.note: Structural gap confirmed - no offside market exists on Smarkets (consistent pattern from USA-PAR). LARGEST single revision in this match (0.42->0.60, +18pp) - the prior qualitative adjustment had the sign backwards, and the corrected direction is corroborated by 3 independent convergent signals (Switzerland's own recency trend, n=10 opponent-vs-Qatar, and the n=3 most-analogous WC2022 subset all point the same way).

018e3215-6bf7-41a4-8344-4ba89e235ace.question: Will Switzerland receive at least 1 card in the second half?
018e3215-6bf7-41a4-8344-4ba89e235ace.estimate: Two competing anchors: (1) Smarkets full-match 'Switzerland over 0.5 cards' mid=0.7271 implies lambda_full~1.298; assuming ~57.5% of cards occur in 2H (general football heuristic) gives lambda_2h~0.746 -> P(>=1)=0.526. (2) ESPN real data: card_rate=0.625/match (lambda_full), only 1/8 matches had a confirmed 2H card -> lambda_2h~0.36 -> P(>=1)~0.30. Blended ~midway, weighting slightly toward the live market since World Cup host-nation matches likely run hotter/more cards than Switzerland's recent friendlies/qualifiers.
018e3215-6bf7-41a4-8344-4ba89e235ace.source: Smarkets full-match cards market (VERIFIED_API, indirect anchor) + ESPN real per-match card data (VERIFIED_API, n=8) + Poisson half-split model
018e3215-6bf7-41a4-8344-4ba89e235ace.note: No direct 2H-team-cards market exists - hybrid of an indirect market anchor and real per-match data, similar in spirit to CAN-BIH Q2's structural-gap modeling. Reliability low because the two anchors diverge by ~23pp (0.526 vs 0.30) and the blend is a judgment call. Session 3: social-media research surfaced a possible referee (Said Martinez, Honduras, ~4.3 YC/game career rate - moderately card-heavy, would support the higher/market-side anchor), but the source had a conflicting kickoff date (listed June 12 vs confirmed June 13) - FLAGGED UNCONFIRMED, not used to move the estimate.

0e47a1b2-97bb-4993-87f8-96fcd9ca774f.question: Will Qatar have 2 or more shots on target in the second half?
0e47a1b2-97bb-4993-87f8-96fcd9ca774f.estimate: REVISED AGAIN (session 3). The WC2022 data adds a directly-relevant n=3 'vs strong opposition' sample of 2H SOT: vs Ecuador=0, vs Senegal=3 (all 3 came in 2H, chasing a 0-2 deficit), vs Netherlands=1. Combined with the existing n=2 (El Salvador=1, Ireland=1), the full n=5 'vs comparable-or-stronger opposition' 2H-SOT sample is [1,1,0,3,1], avg=1.2 -> Poisson(lambda_2h=1.2) gives P(X>=2)=1-e^-1.2*(1+1.2)=0.337. However, the 2026 n=2 subset (avg=1.0) reflects Qatar's CURRENT 'attacking crisis' form (373 min scoreless per social-media research), which the 2022 matches don't capture. Blending the larger-sample signal (0.337) with the current-form signal (n=2 implies ~0.264) and shading slightly toward the latter given recency: lambda_2h~1.05 -> P(X>=2)~0.30.
0e47a1b2-97bb-4993-87f8-96fcd9ca774f.source: ESPN site API (VERIFIED_API, n=2+3, now n=5 total) + Poisson model + social-media current-form context (UNVERIFIED_SEARCH_SUMMARY)
0e47a1b2-97bb-4993-87f8-96fcd9ca774f.note: Structural gap confirmed across Smarkets, Kalshi, Polymarket - no team SOT market (full or half) exists anywhere for Qatar, same pattern as CAN-BIH Q2. REVISED UP from 0.24 - n=5 sample (up from n=2) is more robust; reliability upgraded low->medium. The 2022 Senegal game (3 SOT, all in 2H while trailing) is a useful precedent for 'Qatar generates 2H chances when chasing the game', which is the likely scenario here given Switzerland is heavily favored.

bb71936a-f3b2-471d-acb9-669ab812674e.question: Will Qatar have 2 or more shots on target?
bb71936a-f3b2-471d-acb9-669ab812674e.estimate: REVISED AGAIN (session 3). The WC2022 data adds n=3 'vs strong opposition' full-match SOT: vs Ecuador=0, vs Senegal=3, vs Netherlands=3. Combined with the existing n=2 (El Salvador=1, Ireland=1), the full n=5 'vs comparable-or-stronger opposition' sample is [1,1,0,3,3], avg=1.6 -> Poisson(lambda=1.6) gives P(X>=2)=1-e^-1.6*(1+1.6)=0.475. As with Q5, the 2026 n=2 subset (avg=1.0, P(X>=2)=0.264) reflects Qatar's current 'attacking crisis' (373 min scoreless, social-media research), while the 2022 matches (avg=2.0) reflect a different squad/manager. Blending toward recency but giving the larger sample real weight: lambda~1.35 -> P(X>=2)=1-e^-1.35*(2.35)=0.394.
bb71936a-f3b2-471d-acb9-669ab812674e.source: ESPN site API (VERIFIED_API, n=2+3, now n=5 total) + Poisson model + social-media current-form context (UNVERIFIED_SEARCH_SUMMARY)
bb71936a-f3b2-471d-acb9-669ab812674e.note: Structural gap confirmed across Smarkets, Kalshi, Polymarket - no team SOT market exists anywhere for Qatar. REVISED UP from session-2's 0.28 - n=5 sample (up from n=2) shows a meaningfully higher ceiling vs strong opposition (2022 Senegal/Netherlands both =3 SOT). Reliability upgraded low->medium.

7904c72f-34a8-467f-a973-873cd5953690.question: At halftime, will both teams have at least 1 shot on target?
7904c72f-34a8-467f-a973-873cd5953690.estimate: REVISED (session 3). The WC2022 data adds n=3 1H-SOT values for Qatar vs strong opposition: vs Ecuador=0, vs Senegal=0, vs Netherlands=2. Combined with the existing n=2 (both 0 in 1H), the n=5 1H-SOT sample is [0,0,0,0,2], avg=0.4 -> Poisson(lambda=0.4) gives P(>=1)=1-e^-0.4=0.330. But the 2026 n=2 subset (both 0) reflects current 'attacking crisis' form. Blending the larger sample (0.330) with current-form (~0.22 from n=2 alone): lambda_1h_qatar~0.27 -> P(>=1)~0.236. Switzerland 1H SOT unchanged: lambda_1h_swiss~1.9 -> P(>=1)~0.85. Joint = 0.236 * 0.85 ~ 0.20.
7904c72f-34a8-467f-a973-873cd5953690.source: ESPN site API real per-match data for both teams (VERIFIED_API, n=5+8) + Poisson compound-independence model + social-media current-form context (UNVERIFIED_SEARCH_SUMMARY)
7904c72f-34a8-467f-a973-873cd5953690.note: Structural gap confirmed - no halftime team-SOT market exists on Smarkets. UNCHANGED at 0.20 - the larger n=5 sample (up from n=2) lands almost exactly where the original n=2-based estimate did, a useful convergence check. Reliability upgraded low->medium given the larger sample now corroborates rather than just extrapolates.

post_match_results.beat_crowd_count: 5/10 (Q3, Q4, Q7, Q9, Q10 beat crowd)
post_match_results.source_note: Full postmortem in MATCH_LOG.md - QAT vs SUI postmortem section (corrected for complete final leaderboard, total -39.21)

questions.question: At halftime, will both teams have at least 1 shot on target?
